title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Needle pickup requests in Boston rose 71% from 2022 to 2025, despite being 17% lower than 2020.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% rise in needle pickups from 2020 to 2025, the largest increase in any Boston neighborhood.
The South End, Roxbury, and Dorchester accounted for most requests, while Fenway-Kenmore had a 54% decline over 5 years.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association in honor of her mother.
Cimo reflects on her mother's love language of cooking and the cherished memories formed in their family kitchen.
The marathon run is motivated by Cimo's emotional experiences as her mother battles Alzheimer's disease.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to the Nashville Predators and fell to a 11-14-4 road record this season.
The loss complicates the Bruins' buyer status as they remain in a precarious Eastern Conference Wild Card position.
General Manager Don Sweeney considers trade options, focusing on young assets rather than older rentals before the deadline.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seamus Culleton's deportation paused by U.S. Court of Appeals Chief Judge David Barron pending review of his case.
Culleton, married to a U.S. citizen, has been detained in the ERO East Montana ICE Detention Camp for six months.
Culleton overstayed a visa since 2009 and has a pending green card application; previous drug charges in Ireland reported.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a Foxborough house fire early Tuesday on Granite Street.
The Foxborough Fire Department, aided by Mansfield and Plainville firefighters, contained the fire within an hour.
The cause of the fire remains under investigation by officials, according to fire authorities.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres, increasing coverage to 28.5% from 2019 to 2024.
Growth occurred mostly in public parks and rights-of-way, while some areas like Hyde Park saw declines.
Tree canopy expansion supports Boston's climate goals, including reducing emissions by 2030.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group committed to assist with $7.8 million in security costs for Gillette Stadium's World Cup games.
Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied, citing Kraft Group's proposal as inadequate for addressing the town's security service needs.
BS26 expects state, federal, and commercial funds, but must address concerns to secure an event license by March 17.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway will permanently close this weekend after operating for nearly a decade.
The bar, familiar to Northeastern students, faces closure due to rising insurance costs and declining patronage.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock does not plan to reopen the bar or start a new establishment in Boston.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is listed as 'Questionable' for Friday's Celtics game against the Dallas Mavericks after recovering from a ruptured Achilles.
Coach Joe Mazzulla will adjust Celtics rotations; Tatum expected to play on a minutes restriction, possibly under 30 minutes per game.
Tatum's return may reduce younger players' minutes but could enhance team dynamics with Jaylen Brown as the primary scoring option.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's Achilles' tendon injury occurred during Game 4 of the 2025 Eastern Conference semifinals.
Tatum underwent successful surgery on May 13, 2025, but the Celtics were eliminated by the Knicks without him.
Tatum is expected to return against the Dallas Mavericks at TD Garden after nearly 10 months of rehabilitation.
